commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=24b385ee4e5a11c81bc9c5ae4331617a1111c696)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` schema support, docs, config-resolution plumbing, CLI/embedded runtime threading, and focused tests for scoped compaction behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding: source inspection shows the PR head replaces `AgentToolModelSchema` with `AgentModelSchema` while the existing test still expects `imageGenerationModel.timeoutMs` to parse. I did not run tests because this review must keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor fixes and real behavior proof are needed; automation cannot supply the contributor's external/runtime proof gate for this PR.

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `compaction` and `contextPruning` were effectively global-only. Even after schema work, the active agent's overrides were not consistently threaded through agent config resolution, embedded compaction target selection, and CLI compaction setup.
- Why it matters: mixed-agent deployments often need different context-management policies per agent. A high-context orchestration agent and a cheaper worker agent should not be forced to share the same compaction/pruning behavior.
- What changed:
  - allow and merge per-agent `compaction` / `contextPruning` overrides with `agents.defaults`
  - thread the active `agentId` through embedded-runner and CLI compaction/runtime resolution
  - make embedded compaction target selection honor per-agent provider/model overrides
  - restore parser-backed byte-size validation for compaction transcript limits so existing values like `1.5gb` and `20m` stay valid
  - treat explicit per-agent `compaction.model` overrides as exact, so they do not inherit session model fallback chains
  - resolve per-agent cache-TTL pruning through normalized agent config instead of raw `agents.list` lookup
  - apply merged per-agent compaction settings when preparing PI settings managers
  - add focused tests for agent-scope merging, runtime compaction target selection, exact-model behavior, normalized pruning resolution, and schema validation
  - document the per-agent override behavior
- What did **not** change: the compaction algorithm itself, transport/provider behavior, or unrelated UI/runtime flows. This is a config-resolution and runtime-plumbing feature.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #52732
- Related #52751
- Related #69988
- Additional related compaction/runtime history: #19501, #15826, #43834, #6571, #6572, #30452, #57901, #58077, #59863, #60512, #80784, #82008

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: per-agent `compaction` / `contextPruning` overrides merge with defaults, explicit per-agent `compaction.model` overrides stay exact, parser-supported byte-size config remains valid, and the embedded compaction runtime selects the active agent's compaction target instead of only the global default.
- Real environment tested: local Linux OpenClaw source checkout on branch `feat/agent-compaction-overrides`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.extensions.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true
corepack pnpm exec node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
corepack pnpm exec tsx -e "import { resolveAgentConfig } from './src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts'; import { resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget } from './src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.ts'; import { applyPiCompactionSettingsFromConfig } from './src/agents/pi-settings.ts'; const cfg={agents:{defaults:{compaction:{mode:'default',reserveTokensFloor:20000,keepRecentTokens:30000,model:'openai/gpt-5.4-mini'},contextPruning:{mode:'cache-ttl',ttl:'30m'}},list:[{id:'main',compaction:{mode:'safeguard',provider:'anthropic',model:'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6',qualityGuard:{enabled:true}},contextPruning:{ttl:'2h'}}]}}; const fake={reserve:10000,keep:12000,getCompactionReserveTokens(){return this.reserve;},getCompactionKeepRecentTokens(){return this.keep;},applyOverrides({compaction}){ if (typeof compaction.reserveTokens==='number') this.reserve=compaction.reserveTokens; if (typeof compaction.keepRecentTokens==='number') this.keep=compaction.keepRecentTokens; }}; const applied=applyPiCompactionSettingsFromConfig({settingsManager:fake,cfg,agentId:'main',contextTokenBudget:200000}); console.log(JSON.stringify({resolvedAgent:resolveAgentConfig(cfg,'main'),compactionTarget:resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget({config:cfg,agentId:'main',provider:'openai',modelId:'gpt-5.4',authProfileId:'primary-profile'}),applied},null,2));"
corepack pnpm test src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       84 passed (84)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       9 passed (9)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       26 passed (26)

[plugin-sdk boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[plugin-sdk package boundary dts] fresh; skipping
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 58.1s on 5402 files with 216 rules using 1 threads.

Follow-up review-fix verification:
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 21.17s
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 87.2s on 5402 files with 216 rules using 1 threads.

{
  "resolvedAgent": {
    "compaction": {
      "mode": "safeguard",
      "reserveTokensFloor": 20000,
      "keepRecentTokens": 30000,
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "provider": "anthropic",
      "qualityGuard": {
        "enabled": true
      }
    },
    "contextPruning": {
      "mode": "cache-ttl",
      "ttl": "2h"
    }
  },
  "compactionTarget": {
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  },
  "applied": {
    "didOverride": true,
    "compaction": {
      "reserveTokens": 20000,
      "keepRecentTokens": 30000
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the active agent inherits defaults plus its overrides, the embedded compaction target switches to the per-agent Anthropic override, parser-supported byte-size config remains accepted, explicit per-agent compaction models stay exact without session fallback, and cache-TTL attempt markers respect normalized agent-scoped pruning config.
- What was not tested: a live gateway session performing end-to-end compaction in a real chat/channel run, or the full upstream CI matrix.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: per-agent compaction/context-pruning support was only partially wired. Schema/type acceptance and runtime consumers were not all reading the scoped agent config.
- Missing guardrail: CLI compaction and embedded compaction target selection did not consistently carry `agentId` through the relevant settings/runtime resolution seams.
- Contributing context: the feature spans schema, runtime selection, settings application, and documentation, so partial plumbing still looked superficially complete until typed/runtime seams were exercised together.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should lock this in:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.cache-ttl.test.ts`
  - `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: agent defaults merge with per-agent overrides, embedded compaction target selection uses the active agent's override, explicit per-agent compaction models stay exact instead of inheriting fallback chains, normalized agent IDs still resolve cache-TTL pruning overrides, and the schema keeps the existing parser-backed byte-size contract.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risk is in config-resolution seams and runtime plumbing, so focused tests catch the regression without requiring provider credentials or a full live gateway harness.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can now define `compaction` and `contextPruning` per agent in `agents.list[*]`, with those values merged over `agents.defaults` and honored by the embedded runner / CLI compaction path.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw source checkout
- Model/provider: synthetic local config only for proof; no live provider call
- Integration/channel (if any): embedded runner / CLI compaction config path
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic `agents.defaults` + `agents.list[main]` override fixture shown in the proof command above

### Steps

1. Run the focused agent/runtime/schema tests.
2. Run the plugin-sdk DTS boundary check and bundled-extension lint shard.
3. Run the proof command that resolves scoped agent config, resolves the embedded compaction target, and applies the merged compaction settings to a real settings-manager-compatible object.

### Expected

- Per-agent compaction/context-pruning values merge over defaults.
- Embedded compaction target selection uses the active agent's override.
- Runtime compaction settings apply the merged reserve/keep token values.

### Actual

- Focused tests passed, the typed/lint gates passed, and the proof output shows the active agent resolving to Anthropic for compaction plus merged token settings.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified:

- Verified scenarios: scoped config merge, scoped embedded compaction target resolution, scoped runtime compaction settings application, focused agent/runtime/schema tests, and typed/lint gates covering the touched surfaces.
- Edge cases checked: default-only fallback, per-agent provider/model override, per-agent context-pruning TTL override, and reserve/keep token override application.
- What I did **not** verify: a full live gateway/channel compaction run, external provider calls, or the full CI matrix.

## Review Conversations

N/A - no review conversations addressed yet.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: feature touches multiple config/runtime seams, so partial propagation could leave one execution path still reading only global defaults.
  - Mitigation: cover schema, scoped-config resolution, embedded compaction target selection, PI settings application, and CLI-typed surfaces together.
target.author: kklouzal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
